Apothecary L1 (H).name: APOTHECARY
Apothecary L1 (H).interrupt: Blinding Smoke on Primary 1s and 4s -2 damage / Glowing Shield on Secondary 2s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: GAS CLOUD
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -2 + special - you may not attack next turn

Apothecary L1 (H).flavour: The apothecary wears breeches and a leather tunic which they open to reveal a collection of vials. Pulling one free they throw it at you as they attack.
Apothecary L1 (H).mishap: One of the vials smashes at your feet but nothing happens. You gain an extra attack.
Apothecary L1 (H).prime: They retrieve a larger bottle from their belt. Flames burst up around you. Lose D6 HP.

Artisan L1 (H).name: ARTISAN
Artisan L1 (H).interrupt: Deflect on Secondary 1s -2 damage / Distract on Secondary 6s -1 damage
Artisan L1 (H).flavour: A skilled worker who has spent many years learning their art. They are not a fighter but are well coordinated and wear sturdy leather work clothes. They will defend their home.
Artisan L1 (H).mishap: They grab up a length of wood, but it is brittle and crumbles in their hand. Gain an extra attack.
Artisan L1 (H).prime: The artisan pulls a handful of pins from a pocket and throws them in your face. Take 2 damage.

Blacksmith L1 (H).name: BLACKSMITH
Blacksmith L1 (H).interrupt: Crossed Arms on Secondary 3s and 6s -2 damage
Blacksmith L1 (H).flavour: A hardy artisan, wearing a heavy leather apron, used to hammering metal so they are strong and resistant.
Blacksmith L1 (H).mishap: As the blacksmith attacks they catch their hammer in their apron. You kick out and cause D3 damage.
Blacksmith L1 (H).prime: The blacksmith pulls out a large hook and throws it at you. It catches your arm. Lose D3 HP.

Crazed Preacher L1 (H).name: CRAZED PREACHER
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).flavour: Spreading their vile words in amongst the inhabitants of the dungeon, these crazed preachers are full of fury. They scream and rush towards you.
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).mishap: The wild preacher becomes caught up in their robes and misses a round of combat.
Crazed Preacher L1 (H).prime: They jump forward and somehow latch on to you and are able to perform WILD SCRATCH twice.

Cultist L1 (H).name: CULTIST
Cultist L1 (H).flavour: Serving the religious leaders in the dungeon these worshippers are twisted by hate and set to evil tasks. They wear red robes and face paint, their eyes bulging as they attack.
Cultist L1 (H).mishap: The Cultist pulls a dagger and throws it but it falls at your feet. You throw it back for 2 damage.
Cultist L1 (H).prime: They pull a long chain and fling it at you and wrap it around you arm causing 1 damage.

Dark Cleric L1 (H).name: DARK CLERIC
Dark Cleric L1 (H).interrupt: Dark Magic Haze on Secondary 1s, 2s and 3s - the Dark Cleric heals 1 point of damage if injured and gains one point if not.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).flavour: These brooding magic wielders worship Maduva and harness the power of the dead, turning it upon their enemies. They wear textured grey and black cloaks and padded armour.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).mishap: The cleric swings his staff but misses and slips from their hands. Gain 1 extra attack.
Dark Cleric L1 (H).prime: The air turns cold as the cleric raises their staff. You feel a chill run through you. Lose 2 HP.

Fungal Geist L1 (F).name: FUNGAL GEIST
Fungal Geist L1 (F).flavour: A squat fungal humanoid, part mushroom part unknown beast. Their appearance can vary, but they usually have a wide textured cap and small mushrooms sometimes cover their torso.
Fungal Geist L1 (F).mishap: The geist attempts a volley of kicks all of which fail short allowing you to prod it hard causing 2 damage.
Fungal Geist L1 (F).prime: The geist raises its cap revealing narrow gills from which it fires a series of darts. Lose D3 HP.

Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).name: GIANT HORNED ANACONDA
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).interrupt: Scale block on Secondary 2s and 5s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: SMOTHER
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -2 damage + special attack - miss next round (once per combat).

Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).flavour: A large brown snake with rock like horned ridges, it uses to attack aggressors. They live in rocky environments often near dungeons or caves, where their appearance is perfect camouflage.
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).mishap: The snake snaps back and tries to bash you with its head, but instead smashes the wall for D3 damage.
Giant Horned Anaconda L1 (A).prime: The snake rises high on its tail and swoops down performing a successful HORN JAB.

Guard L1 (H).name: GUARD
Guard L1 (H).interrupt: Shield Block on Secondary 3s and 5s -1 damage
Guard L1 (H).flavour: A trained guard wearing light armour who has some basic skills in combat. They have duties within the dungeon and can be seen on patrols and at guard posts.
Guard L1 (H).mishap: The guard lunges carelessly, opens his side and you elbow them hard causing 1 damage.
Guard L1 (H).prime: With an unexpected move the guard disarms you. Lose 1 turn as you grab it back up off the ground.

Guard Dog L1 (A).name: GUARD DOG
Guard Dog L1 (A).flavour: A trained guard dog, appears vicious but has little experience fighting.
Guard Dog L1 (A).mishap: The dog skids as it lunges and you are able to get in an extra attack with +1.
Guard Dog L1 (A).prime: The dog thrusts forward and clamps its jaws around your arm causing D3 damage.

Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).name: HUGE INFECTED RAT

manoeuvres.name: INFECTED BITE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -3 damage + special - next two turns lose 1 HP

Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).flavour: A large grim looking rodent covered in pus-filled blisters that appears to be in a rabid state. Its red eyes bulge as it screeches and attacks.
Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).mishap: The creature is sickly and seems to cough and spits blood causing it D3 damage.
Huge Infected Rat L1 (A).prime: The rat evades your defences and scurries up your leg and bites your neck using INFECTED BITE.

Huge Rat L1 (A).name: HUGE RAT
Huge Rat L1 (A).treasure: A rat tail without becoming bloodied.
Huge Rat L1 (A).flavour: Not a giant but bigger than a normal rat that will savagely attack anything, even larger creatures, when hungry. They are always hungry.
Huge Rat L1 (A).mishap: The rat attacks but slips and rolls on its back. You bring down your boot and crush its head, killing it.
Huge Rat L1 (A).prime: The rat pounces and lands on your face. It bites you before you brush it off. Lose 1 HP.

Huge Spider L1 (I).name: HUGE SPIDER
Huge Spider L1 (I).flavour: Not a giant but bigger than a normal spider with a tough carapace and enlarged fangs. It will attack if something breaks its web.
Huge Spider L1 (I).mishap: The spider falls as its web breaks and you spin and slice it in two, killing it instantly.
Huge Spider L1 (I).prime: The spider jumps, spins a web, and pings onto your back where it sinks in its fangs. Lose 1 HP.

Infernal Monk L1 (H).name: INFERNAL MONK
Infernal Monk L1 (H).interrupt: Heat Wave on Primary 2s and 5s -1 damage
Infernal Monk L1 (H).flavour: Dressed in red leather bands and black clothes the bald monk can call upon Grakada to summon fire that extends from their arm as an infernal whip. It lashes towards you as they attack.
Infernal Monk L1 (H).mishap: Their whip lashes the air and flings back in their face causing them 2 damage.
Infernal Monk L1 (H).prime: A second fire whip appears on their other hand and together they strike you. Lose 3 HP.

Jailor L1 (H).name: JAILOR
Jailor L1 (H).flavour: Being a jailor has made them tough but their skill in combat is limited. As they come forward wrapping a chain around their knuckles, a mad look in their eyes.
Jailor L1 (H).mishap: The jailor swings but appears tired and falls back on their haunches giving you an extra attack.
Jailor L1 (H).prime: The chain shoots out and lashes your knuckles. Lose 1 HP if not wearing gloves.

Labourer L1 (H).name: LABOURER
Labourer L1 (H).flavour: Poorly equipped and unarmed, this labourer offers little challenge, but they have learned patience and do a range of jobs around the dungeon which they will fiercely defend.
Labourer L1 (H).mishap: The labourer flays wildly and loses their balance. You quickly counter gaining an extra attack.
Labourer L1 (H).prime: The labourer barrels past your defence swinging wildly and a punch lands. Take 1 damage.

Medic L1 (H).name: MEDIC
Medic L1 (H).interrupt: Nimble Move on Primary 3s and 4s -1 damage
Medic L1 (H).flavour: The nearest thing the dungeon has to a herbalist medic who looks after the injured but has very little skill. From a bloody apron they pull out a short knife and attack.
Medic L1 (H).mishap: There must be blood on the medic's shoes for they lose their footing and fall. Gain 1 extra attack.
Medic L1 (H).prime: The medic whips the apron free, throws it over your head and stabs you causing 2 damage.

Merchant L1 (H).name: MERCHANT
Merchant L1 (H).treasure: A wallet with D6 GC and D6 SC
Merchant L1 (H).interrupt: Divert Blow on Primary 2s and 5s -1 damage
Merchant L1 (H).flavour: Dressed in fine clothes the merchant manages trade in the dungeon and makes a profit doing so. They pull out a curved sword and prepare to defend their position.
Merchant L1 (H).mishap: They try a flurry of complex swings, trip and slash their own leg. They take one damage.
Merchant L1 (H).prime: The merchant throws a potion at you that explodes. It causes D3 damage.

Musty Bloater L1 (F).name: MUSTY BLOATER
Musty Bloater L1 (F).interrupt: Blinding Spore Cloud on Primary 3s, 4s and 5s -1 damage
Musty Bloater L1 (F).flavour: Animated by primordial magic these swollen fungi shoot hot spores at their victims. They lumber forward, rising up and swelling, from what appears to be flat mould or mildew.
Musty Bloater L1 (F).mishap: The creature seems to lose some of its mass and shrinks for a moment. It misses a round of combat.
Musty Bloater L1 (F).prime: Suddenly the Bloater pressure shoots out a shower of spores. Lose D3 HP.

Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).name: SCARLET COBRA
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).interrupt: Darts Aside on Primary 2s and 3s -1 damage

manoeuvres.name: BITE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -3 damage + special attack - poisoned - lose 1 HP per round for 2 rounds.

Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).flavour: A poisonous black snake with scarlet red banding. Lives in deep burrows sometimes finding its way down through cracks in the walls and ceiling of the dungeon.
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).mishap: The snake shoots out its head in an attempted bite, but misses and you're able to slash it for D3 damage.
Scarlet Cobra L1 (A).prime: The cobra corners coils and whips forward past your defence successfully biting you, see BITE.

Scout L1 (H).name: SCOUT
Scout L1 (H).interrupt: Swift Jump on Primary 1s and 6s -2 damage
Scout L1 (H).flavour: Usually out of the dungeon scouting the area they strive to avoid combat but are not afraid of a fight. Dressed in green and agile on their feet they thrust the point of a dagger towards you.
Scout L1 (H).mishap: The scout tries to grab your weapon but instead twists their wrist and takes 1 damage.
Scout L1 (H).prime: The scout leaps back, pulls out a short bow and looses an arrow. Take D3 damage.

Shard Rock Python L1 (A).name: SHARD ROCK PYTHON

manoeuvres.name: STRANGLE
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -1 damage + special attack - when you attack next round you do one less damage.

Shard Rock Python L1 (A).flavour: A heavy snake, with textured rough skin that is a grey rock colour. It wraps around its victims and strangles the life out of them.
Shard Rock Python L1 (A).mishap: The snake's slow attack allows you to bash it on the head as it tries to move in closer causing D6 damage.
Shard Rock Python L1 (A).prime: The python corners you, wraps it's bulk around your leg causing lose D6 HP.

Slimy Gorger L1 (F).name: SLIMY GORGER
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).interrupt: Gelatinous Body on Secondary 2s and 6s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: STICKY SUCTION
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -1 damage + special attack - you may not use one random piece of armour this turn.

Slimy Gorger L1 (F).flavour: A dark green slime animated by primordial magic, that lives in wet and cold abandoned spaces. It can rear up and attack with its stretchy gelatinous body.
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).mishap: The slime hisses, but cannot take an attacking form and misses a round of combat.
Slimy Gorger L1 (F).prime: The slime swells into a wide sheet of green that engulfs you. You struggle free but lose 2 HP.

Thug L1 (H).name: THUG
Thug L1 (H).flavour: This rough looking brute has few skills and little training and is often deployed as muscle to do unsavoury jobs. They shout abuse as they attack swinging their fists.
Thug L1 (H).mishap: The thug tries a combo of swing and uppercut but misses and topples over. Gain 1 extra attack.
Thug L1 (H).prime: The thug manages to grip and up eyes wide they headbutt you hard, take D3 damage.

Veteran L1 (H).name: VETERAN
Veteran L1 (H).treasure: Roll on PT1 +1 and an extra D6 GC
Veteran L1 (H).interrupt: Parry on Primary 3s and 4s -2 damage / Armour deflection on Secondary 5s and 6s -1 damage
Veteran L1 (H).flavour: A strong and experienced old fighter, who used to be a soldier, and knows how to handle themselves. They appear grizzled and slow but can parry efficiently. They sneer as they attack.
Veteran L1 (H).mishap: The old veteran stumbles and falls. Gain 2 extra hits as he tries to rise.
Veteran L1 (H).prime: Out of nowhere the veteran twists and performs a successful CRUSHING BLOW.

War Hound L1 (A).name: WAR HOUND
War Hound L1 (A).interrupt: Raised Legs on Primary 5s and 6s -2 damage
War Hound L1 (A).flavour: A vicious dog trained to fight. Wears a spiked collar it uses in its charge attack.
War Hound L1 (A).mishap: The dog seems keen on simply barking in an attempt to scare you and misses the next round.
War Hound L1 (A).prime: The dog barrels into you dragging its spiked collar down your thigh causing D6 damage.

Warrior L1 (H).name: WARRIOR
Warrior L1 (H).interrupt: Bracer block on Primary 4s -1 damage / Dodge on Secondary 3s - 1 damage
Warrior L1 (H).flavour: A capable warrior trained in the art of combat. They move quickly and  appear well equipped. They wear thick metal bracers which they can use to block attacks.
Warrior L1 (H).mishap: The warrior swings but loses his balance and you kick them hard in the ribs causing D3 damage.
Warrior L1 (H).prime: The warrior pulls a small dagger from his boot and flicks it at you. It glances off your cheek. Lose 1 HP.

Work Dog L1 (A).name: WORK DOG
Work Dog L1 (A).flavour: A working dog with a need to defend its owner, and although not trained to fight still has a savage bite.
Work Dog L1 (A).mishap: The dog flinches and slips as it comes forward afraid of your attack. It hits the wall taking 1 damage.
Work Dog L1 (A).prime: The dog wrestles through your defence and slashes you with a paw. Lose 1 HP.

Handler L1 (H).name: HANDLER
Handler L1 (H).treasure: A pouch full of biscuit chips.
Handler L1 (H).interrupt: Leather Straps on Secondary 4s and 6s -1 damage
Handler L1 (H).flavour: The handler appears dirty and their clothes worn and muddy. Dealing with the dogs and cleaning out the kennels is their primary role which is clear from their apperance. They carry a whip.
Handler L1 (H).mishap: Caught in two minds, an inexperienced fighter, the handler hesitates and misses their next attack.
Handler L1 (H).prime: The handler whoops and cracks their whip. Like a flash you feel it lash your cheek and lose 1 HP.

Adept L2 (H).name: ADEPT
Adept L2 (H).interrupt: Quick Block on Secondary 2s and 5s -2 damage
Adept L2 (H).flavour: Adepts are the trusted managers of the dungeon, organising the staff and minions. They paint red slashes on their faces, wear grey robes and carry short staffs.
Adept L2 (H).mishap: The Adept seems to be strained and falls back. You gain +1 Shift on the next attack.
Adept L2 (H).prime: They pull a small vial free and fling it. It strikes and you seem to slow down. -1 Shift for the next round.

Amaurotic L2 (H).name: AMAUROTIC
Amaurotic L2 (H).interrupt: Sonic Block on Primary 1s, 2s and 6s -1 damage
Amaurotic L2 (H).flavour: Blinded and insane the Amaurotic have a heightened sense of hearing. Guided by Nevazator they wander down to the crypt levels to avoid the persecution of domain guards.
Amaurotic L2 (H).mishap: The Amaurotic catches the wall with their staff, jarring their wrist, losing 3 HP.
Amaurotic L2 (H).prime: They shoot their staff forward through your guard striking your chest. Lose 3 HP

Banished L2 (H).name: BANISHED
Banished L2 (H).interrupt: Scurry Away on Secondary 1s, 4s, 5s and 6s -1 damage
Banished L2 (H).flavour: Banished from the Domain level to the crypt levels, for heinous crimes, these tortured beings are renowned grisly cannibals, desperate but unable to escape the domain of the dead.
Banished L2 (H).mishap: They cower away from your attack suddenly scared, shrinking back. You +2 Shift in the next round.
Banished L2 (H).prime: The Banished dives to the ground, grabs your leg and sink in their teeth. Lose 2 HP.

Baric Knight L2 (H).name: BARIC KNIGHT
Baric Knight L2 (H).interrupt: Shoulder Guards on Primary 1s -2 damage / Helm on Secondary 1s -1 damage / Banded Chestplate on Secondary 2s and 3s -2 damage
Baric Knight L2 (H).flavour: Part of the Baric Order, the well armoured Baric Knights, are a group of trained warriors who serve the Overlord and follow an oath to protect the subterranean world that which is above.
Baric Knight L2 (H).mishap: Slowed by their armour the knight stumbles and does not attack this turn as they gain their balance.
Baric Knight L2 (H).prime: A mighty blow comes down from the Knight's blade, destroying one random piece of armour (not plate).

Brown Bear L2 (A).name: BROWN BEAR
Brown Bear L2 (A).treasure: A set of fangs without becoming bloodied
Brown Bear L2 (A).flavour: Often hunted and trapped these aggressive creatures are used in terrible bear baiting events in the dungeon. They are trained to attack anyone but their master.
Brown Bear L2 (A).mishap: The bear throws its body forward, stumbles and you catch it with your weapon causing D6 damage.
Brown Bear L2 (A).prime: The bear lands on top of you and bites your shoulder causing D6 damage. You roll free.

Giant Rat L2 (A).name: GIANT RAT
Giant Rat L2 (A).treasure: A rat tail without becoming bloodied
Giant Rat L2 (A).flavour: The rats in the crypt levels have been affected by the grim soup of magic and toxins that pollute the many dark places. Lusting for fresh meat they attack when they see you come.
Giant Rat L2 (A).mishap: The rat dives forward but misses and slides across the space. You lash out and do 3 HP damage.
Giant Rat L2 (A).prime: The rat jumps and swings a clawed paw, catching your chin, drawing blood. You lose 2 HP.

Heedless L2 (H).name: HEEDLESS
Heedless L2 (H).interrupt: Leather Armour on Secondary 1s, 2s, 5s and 6s -2 damage
Heedless L2 (H).flavour: Heedless are hardy folk who spend time in the crypt levels of the dungeon searching for loved ones or lost treasure, avoiding the undead and sleeping in empty sarcophagi.
Heedless L2 (H).mishap: Their baton slips from their hand. Gain an extra attack while they recover it from the ground.
Heedless L2 (H).prime: The Heedless swings into a frenzy, gaining +2 Shift and +2 damage on their next attack.

Mercenary L2 (H).name: MERCENARY
Mercenary L2 (H).interrupt: Breastplate on Primary 2s and and 5s -2 damage
Mercenary L2 (H).flavour: These well armoured, hired hands have embedded themselves in the running of the dungeon, but remain independent. They are skilled warriors and know it.
Mercenary L2 (H).mishap: Too confident, their attack is casual allowing you to swipe their side causing D3 damage.
Mercenary L2 (H).prime: Quick with their sword the Mercenary spins and pushes forward quickly with a jab. Lose D3 HP.

Mystic L2 (H).name: MYSTIC
Mystic L2 (H).interrupt: Ethereal Body on Secondary 2s, 3s, 4s and 5s -1 damage
Mystic L2 (H).flavour: One of Intuneric's servants, the Mystic, draws upon the unseen and the darkness. They seem to shimmer as if phasing in and out of reality and cast wisps of smoke from their hands.
Mystic L2 (H).mishap: The Mystic falls to their knees and screams. They are prone and you swing hard causing D6 damage.
Mystic L2 (H).prime: A swirling mass of black chums around the mystic, forms a spear and strikes you. Lose D6 HP.

Overlord L2 (H).name: OVERLORD
Overlord L2 (H).interrupt: Padded Robes on Primary 2s -1 damage / Forearm Block on Secondry 4s and 5s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: BEAR HUG
manoeuvres.formula: D6 -2 damage + special attack - random potion is destroyed (once per combat)

Overlord L2 (H).flavour: The Overlord runs the top two levels of the dungeon, the Baric Knights carrying out duties on their behalf. The Overlord is a tough adversary, who is never afraid to get their hands dirty.
Overlord L2 (H).mishap: The Overlord stumbles on a piece of furniture, their great bulk a hinderance so cannot attack this turn.
Overlord L2 (H).prime: The Overlord manages to grip your elbow with their massive hand and twists it. Take D6 damage.

Skeleton Warrior L2 (U).name: SKELETON WARRIOR
Skeleton Warrior L2 (U).interrupt: Body Void on Primary 3s and 4s -1 damage
Skeleton Warrior L2 (U).flavour: The skeleton rises up its the black eye sockets empty voids. This animated warrior is held together by a dark magic and their singular purpose is to kill the living. It advances.
Skeleton Warrior L2 (U).mishap: The armour on the skeleton makes it top heavy and it trips. You kick it hard for D3 damage.
Skeleton Warrior L2 (U).prime: The skeleton peels off a chunk of armour, flings it fast and high and it hits you. Lose D3 HP.

Sporal Rapnel L2 (F).name: SPORAL RAPNEL
Sporal Rapnel L2 (F).interrupt: Spore Screen on Primary 3s -1 damage / Tough Hide on Secondary 1s and 6s -2 damage
Sporal Rapnel L2 (F).flavour: From a pulsing mass of fungi rises a large head, as if a giant sunflower. It has a central mouth, throbbing with red spores, surrounded by green tentacles. It rattles as if a snake and attacks.
Sporal Rapnel L2 (F).mishap: Part of the Sporal Rapnel base rips free as it tries to swing, causing it D6 damage as it writhes.
Sporal Rapnel L2 (F).prime: The head of the creature pulls back vibrating as a mass of spores shoots free to blast you. Lose D6 HP.

The Vanished L2 (U).name: THE VANISHED
The Vanished L2 (U).interrupt: Dark Aura on Secondary 4s and 6s -2 damage / Head Mask on Primary 1s -1 damage
The Vanished L2 (U).flavour: The Vanished are rumoured to be the wayward souls who have disappeared from the surface and end up corrupted by the same dark magic that animates the dead. They hide in the shadows.
The Vanished L2 (U).mishap: Black puss bursts from under its mask, making it fall back screaming and miss the next round.
The Vanished L2 (U).prime: The Vanished reaches down, grabs a stone and throws it, glancing your head. Lose 2 HP.

Zombie L2 (U).name: ZOMBIE
Zombie L2 (U).interrupt: Lifeless Corpse on Secondary 1s, 2s and 6s -1 damage
Zombie L2 (U).flavour: The zombie turns in your direction, the flesh loose on its face, a blank terror suspended in the white globes that are its eyes. It moans and lopes towards you.
Zombie L2 (U).mishap: A large chunk of the creature's arm falls away as it swings causing it D6 damage.
Zombie L2 (U).prime: The creature stumbles and throws its weight at you, knocking you aside. Lose 2 HP.

Afflicted L3 (H).name: AFFLICTED
Afflicted L3 (H).interrupt: Tough Skin on Primary 1s, 2s, 5s and 6s -1 damage
Afflicted L3 (H).flavour: Sometimes experimenting with Metamorphic magic can warp the body as well as the mind resulting in the Afflicted. No threat to the Order they attack those who they do not recognise.
Afflicted L3 (H).mishap: Their crazed attack leaves them open allowing you to strike low and cause 2D6 damage to an exposed leg.
Afflicted L3 (H).prime: The Afflicted pulls a sharp bone from their less and flicks it at you. It sticks in your shoulder. Lose 4 HP.

Ashen Fext L3 (U).name: ASHEN FEXT
Ashen Fext L3 (U).interrupt: Tough Hide on Primary 3s, 4s and 5s -2 damage
Ashen Fext L3 (U).flavour: Ashen Souls are undead touched by Maduva yet their bodies are preserved and filled with metamorphic energy as they flux between death and life, the imbalance driving them insane.
Ashen Fext L3 (U).mishap: The creature seems paralysed. Their Interrupt stats have no effect in the next round of combat.
Ashen Fext L3 (U).prime: The Fext raises a whirling hand forming a circular shield. You lose 2 Shift in the next round of combat.

Basal Initiate L3 (H).name: BASAL INITIATE
Basal Initiate L3 (H).interrupt: Side Step on Primary 3s and 5s -2 damage / Heat Shield on Secondary 4s -3 damage
Basal Initiate L3 (H).flavour: The Basal Initiates were once Infernal Monks who have been drawn into the study of Primordial magic. They have one goal in the maniacal studies and that is to become a Reveled Axiom.
Basal Initiate L3 (H).mishap: The initiate tries to draw upon their magic but falls giving you a chance to throw a weapon.
Basal Initiate L3 (H).prime: The initiate clicks their fingers and fire bursts from the ground all around you. Lose 2D6 HP.

Faraviata L3 (U).name: FARAVIATA

manoeuvres.name: LOCKED STARE
manoeuvres.formula: D3 damage + special attack - Next round deduct 1 shift

Faraviata L3 (U).flavour: The Faraviata are warped humans who have been drained of life through infernal worship. Yet they return to reinhabit their previous bodies and are now animalian, bestial, hungry for blood.
Faraviata L3 (U).mishap: The creature gulps up a rat and sinks in its fangs. Chance presented you swipe doing D6 damage.
Faraviata L3 (U).prime: The Faraviata leaps forward past your guard and sinks its teeth into your neck. Lose D6 HP.

Ghost L3 (U).name: GHOST
Ghost L3 (U).interrupt: Static Fusion on Primary 2s and 3s -2 damage / Phased Arm on Secondary 6's -2 damage
Ghost L3 (U).flavour: Ghosts appear in the form of their past life surround by white astral energies, that channels their ferocity into the whole. They viciously want revenge on all the living.
Ghost L3 (U).mishap: The rise up in the air and seem to be trying to shielding their attack. They lose the next turn.
Ghost L3 (U).prime: The ghost raises its glowing hands and fires a bolt of energy at you. Lose 3 HP.

Grey Anchorite L3 (H).name: GREY ANCHORITE
Grey Anchorite L3 (H).interrupt: Wild Dodge on Primary 1s and 6s -2 damage
Grey Anchorite L3 (H).flavour: Although Grey Anchorites do many of the chores in the infernal levels they are also aspirational and have a wild fervour. But they lack the talent to progress and will often remain in the grey.
Grey Anchorite L3 (H).mishap: They become tangled in their grey robes and miss the next round of combat as they free themselves.
Grey Anchorite L3 (H).prime: They cast a scream of sparks that wash across you, stinging as they touch your skin. Lose D6 +1 HP.

Mummy L3 (U).name: MUMMY
Mummy L3 (U).interrupt: Senseless Body on Primary 1s, and 6s -3 damage
Mummy L3 (U).flavour: Many of the bodies in the crypt have been embalmed and, now risen, roam the dark halls as rigid mummies. It turns and senses you, the dry corpse visible beneath the strips of cloth.
Mummy L3 (U).mishap: One of the pieces of cloth becomes tangled and it trips, hitting its head on a ledge. It loses 3 HP.
Mummy L3 (U).prime: The mummy seems less affected by your weapon. You have -2 damage on your next hit.

Oldfang Pillarist L3 (H).name: OLDFANG PILLARIST
Oldfang Pillarist L3 (H).interrupt: Breast Plate on Primary 5s and 6s -2 damage / Forearm Block on Secondary 2s -1 damage
Oldfang Pillarist L3 (H).flavour: Once Red Guard the Oldfangs have taken on the role of command as time takes its toll. But they are still as tough as always and formidable opponents, despite being somewhat slower.
Oldfang Pillarist L3 (H).mishap: The Oldfang pauses and backs away and seems to rest against the wall. They miss the next turn.
Oldfang Pillarist L3 (H).prime: The Oldfang unexpectedly twirls and thrusts arcs down towards you. You cant not avoid it. Lose 2D3 HP.

Puppeter L3 (H).name: PUPPETEER
Puppeter L3 (H).treasure: Roll on PT2 +2 + a golden ring worth 2D6
Puppeter L3 (H).interrupt: Control Movement on Primary 2s, 3s and 4s -1 damage + special defence - the Puppeteer controls your next attack reducing the damage by 2.
Puppeter L3 (H).flavour: Puppeteers for the Infernal Order recruiting and bringing in new blood to the dungeon. They manipulate the Psyche Orbit and control those who they deem unworthy.
Puppeter L3 (H).mishap: They seem to focus on you but feel nothing, their magic fails. You charge them causing D6 damage.
Puppeter L3 (H).prime: You sense the magic grip you and turn and smash your head into the wall. Lose D6 HP.

Shadow Blade L3 (H).name: SHADOW BLADE
Shadow Blade L3 (H).interrupt: Dodge Backwards on Primary 1s and 6s -2 damage + special defense - the Shadow Blade lashes out as they dodge back and catches you. Lose 1 HP.
Shadow Blade L3 (H).flavour: Some thieves and brigands are chosen by the Infernal Order and are trained to draw upon the Faculty Orbit. They use this magic to hone their dexterous skills and serve as spies and assassins.
Shadow Blade L3 (H).mishap: The Shadow Blade turns and throws their dagger but it sticks in the wall. They miss the next turn.
Shadow Blade L3 (H).prime: They punch you and a blade shoots from their wrist striking deep cutting you deeply. Lose D3 +2 HP.

Stone Maiden L3 (U).name: STONE MAIDEN
Stone Maiden L3 (U).interrupt: Stone Skin on Primary 3s, 4s and 5s -2 damage
Stone Maiden L3 (U).flavour: Stone Maidens are favoured by Grakada and were once women sacrificed by the Infernal Monks and placed in tombs, only to reappear with stoney skin and a lust for life.
Stone Maiden L3 (U).mishap: The Maiden becomes slowed and stonelike. They lose 1 shift for the next round.
Stone Maiden L3 (U).prime: The Maiden fires two shards from her open palm. Both hit. Lose D3 and D3 HP.

Tomb Raider L3 (H).name: TOMB RAIDER
Tomb Raider L3 (H).treasure: Roll on BST2 and BT2 +2 and +2D6 GC
Tomb Raider L3 (H).interrupt: Low Duck on Primary 4s and 5s -2 damage / Forearm Block on Secondary 1s and 2s -1 damage
Tomb Raider L3 (H).flavour: There are a few people who dare to venture into the crypts in search of treasure. They are more than happy to recover the treasure from those who get in their way. They draw a knife and attack.
Tomb Raider L3 (H).mishap: Hampered by their booty the Tomb Raider loses 1 Shift for the next round of combat.
Tomb Raider L3 (H).prime: They pull out a spiked chain and flick it at you. It loops around and lashes your arm. Lose 2 HP.

Undead Bear L3 (U).name: UNDEAD BEAR
Undead Bear L3 (U).interrupt: Senseless Hide on Primary 2s, and 6s -1 damage
Undead Bear L3 (U).flavour: Bears that escape the baiters flee into the crypts only to be fed on by the Faraviata and once drained of blood are reanimated as undead bears, their fur bleached white by death.
Undead Bear L3 (U).mishap: The undead bear tries to barge you but slips and tumbles to the ground, missing the next round.
Undead Bear L3 (U).prime: Long claws snap the straps on your armour. It must be repaired before it can be used again.

Banshee L4 (U).name: BANSHEE
Banshee L4 (U).interrupt: Resonating Barrier on Primary 1s, 2s and 6s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: VIBRO PUNCH
manoeuvres.formula: D3 +1 damage + special attack - Shift -1 next round

Banshee L4 (U).flavour: Banshees are twisted by the Orbit of Psyche. They are beings who tormented by their own minds or are sent away into the depths where they cannot disturb those in the domain above.
Banshee L4 (U).mishap: The Banshee seems tormented, grabbing their head, giving you an extra attack next round.
Banshee L4 (U).prime: The Banshee opens her mouth and screams. The sonic wave hits you. Lose 4 HP.

Ofilit L4 (U).name: OFILIT
Ofilit L4 (U).treasure: Roll on BST2+1 + a torque worth 3D6 GC
Ofilit L4 (U).interrupt: Padded Shoulder on Primary 5s -2 damage / Swinging Forearm on Secondary 3s and 6s -1 damage
Ofilit L4 (U).flavour: Ofilit are ancient giants of the old world, returned to the deep caves as undead beings, only to find the dungeon in its place. They wander the tunnels confused, aggressively seeking their lost home.
Ofilit L4 (U).mishap: The Ofilit wildly swings its fist against the wall, and some stone comes down on it causing 2D3 damage.
Ofilit L4 (U).prime: The Ofilit charges, its oddly domed skull striking you hard in the chest, winding you, causing 2D3 damage.

Under Troll L4 (C).name: UNDER TROLL
Under Troll L4 (C).interrupt: Tough Skin on Secondary 1s, 2s, 3s, 4s and 6s -1 damage

manoeuvres.name: ROCK THROW
manoeuvres.formula: D3 damage + special attack - 1 random, carried large item is lost

Under Troll L4 (C).flavour: When the dungeon was being made a number of ancient Under Trolls were discovered and trapped. Later these angry creatures escaped into the lower levels and now hunt for fresh meat.
Under Troll L4 (C).mishap: The Troll is blinded by a flash of light. A big target, you lunge in and strike it hard for D6 damage.
Under Troll L4 (C).prime: The Troll grips your arm and twists it. Now in pain, you lose 1 shift for the next two combat rounds.

Red Guard L4 (H).name: RED GUARD
Red Guard L4 (H).interrupt: Plate Armour on Primary 2s, 3s and 5s -2 damage
Red Guard L4 (H).flavour: The Red Guard are elite warriors raised from childhood and trained by the Infernal Order. They are taught to guard the lower levels and serve the Mordeel and the Infernal Cardinal.
Red Guard L4 (H).mishap: The Red Guard stumbles and misses their swing. You are able to counter and cause D6 damage.
Red Guard L4 (H).prime: The Red Guard pulls a dagger and flings it at you and it slices past your face. Lose D6 +2 HP.

Zordian Shade L4 (U).name: ZORDIAN SHADE
Zordian Shade L4 (U).interrupt: Agile Leap on Primary 1s, 2s, and 6s -1 damage / Muscular Frame on Secondary 1s and 2s -2 damage

manoeuvres.name: GAS ROAR
manoeuvres.formula: D3 damage + special attack - next combat round -1 Shift.

Zordian Shade L4 (U).flavour: Zordian Shades are possessed corpses of a species of extinct lion, scourged by primordial magic. They hate humans and seek revenge for the persecution they suffered in life. It roars!
Zordian Shade L4 (U).mishap: It seems to want to destroy a piece of furniture in its rage and misses the next round.
Zordian Shade L4 (U).prime: The Zordian Shade pounces and jumps high across the room, to land on top of you. Lose D6 HP.
